message:
feat(websocket): send appropriate RFC 6455 close codes before error returns and add token-list upgrade test (bd-z09e)

Harden WebSocket error paths to send the correct close frame code before
returning an error, per RFC 6455 Section 7.4.1:

- 1002 (Protocol Error): already sent for malformed frames and invalid
  close payloads; now also sent for unexpected continuation frames and
  new-text-before-completion errors
- 1003 (Unsupported Data): sent when a binary frame arrives in a
  text-only read path
- 1007 (Invalid Payload Data): sent when text or reassembled continuation
  frames contain invalid UTF-8
- 1009 (Message Too Big): sent when text message or continuation
  accumulation exceeds MAX_TEXT_MESSAGE_BYTES

Refactor: extract send_close_code() helper to eliminate duplicated
Frame construction for close frames across 6 call sites.

New integration test: websocket_upgrade_with_token_list_headers_and_echo_text
validates that comma-separated Upgrade ("h2c, websocket") and Connection
("keep-alive, Upgrade") headers are correctly parsed during the WebSocket
handshake, followed by a text echo round-trip.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
